content.full_text:
December 3, 2002

J. Dennis Crabb
Rollston, Henderson, Rasmussen & Crabb
591 Tahoe Keys Boulevard, Suite D8
South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150

Re: Your Requests for Advice
Our File Nos. 02-322; 02-329

Dear Mr. Crabb:

This letter confirms the receipt of your October 21, 2002 and November 25, 2002 requests for advice regarding Mayor Keith M. Sweet and the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Despite your supplemental correspondence, the FPPC still considers your letters of inquiry as hypothetical, which under enclosed regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), is a circumstance in which we decline to provide written advice. Additionally, execution of the resolution in question would be considered past conduct, another exception to regulation 18329 wherein the FPPC declines to render advice.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5660 should you have additional questions regarding this correspondence.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosure
JWW:jg

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
